title: Monotone constrained pit-pressure expert
summary: Several current features encode obvious directional priors: more finish-pressure, more stop-debt, and worse positive lap-time drift should usually not reduce pit likelihood ceteris paribus.
rationale: Several current features encode obvious directional priors: more finish-pressure, more stop-debt, and worse positive lap-time drift should usually not reduce pit likelihood ceteris paribus. Monotone constraints can regularize tree models toward physically plausible behavior and sometimes improve generalization.
implementation_hint: Train a separate XGBoost or LightGBM expert with monotonic constraints on a small trusted subset of features: increasing on TyreLife_ToFinish pressure, degradation-per-life, positive LapTime_Delta, prior mandatory-stop debt, and late-race pressure; decreasing on fresh-tyre indicators or finish margin. Blend this constrained expert with the unconstrained model.
expected_effect: Likely small but robust gain, especially on public LB/OOD races where unconstrained trees overfit spurious local reversals.
risk: Wrong-sign constraints on noisy features can cap upside. Apply only to features with strong domain directionality.
